rationale: The citing paper uses Sethuraman, Tellis, and Briesch (2011) for more than a background mention. It explicitly benchmarks its own ad-response findings as "small but generally positive" against that prior meta-analytic evidence, and it also uses a specific generalization from that paper (long-term effects about twice short-term effects) to construct long-term simulation outcomes. This indicates the cited work informs both interpretation of results and a key modeling assumption in the counterfactual analysis.
relation_description: The citing study applies empirical generalizations from Sethuraman et al. (2011) to its own TV targeting context. In particular, it uses that meta-analysis as a benchmark for expected ad-effect magnitude and as an input assumption when mapping short-term to long-term advertising effects in simulations.
